title: Jyväskylä Longitudinal Study of Personality and Social Development (JYLS): Teacher Ratings, Peer Nominations and School Reports of 14-Year-Olds 1974
description: The data are part of the Finnish Jyväskylä Longitudinal Study of Personality and Social Development (JYLS), in which the same individuals have been followed over 30 years. At this research wave, 14-year-olds' social behaviour and living circumstances were explored. The data were collected using teacher ratings and peer nominations, in a similar fashion as at the first data collection wave, when the respondents were 8 years old.
